title: The Old Man and His Sons
author: Heðin Brú

setting.note: The old fisherman Ketil, of the self-sufficient traditional generation, gets carried away at a whale auction and buys more meat than he can pay for, and the debt sends him and his wife back to the hard old ways to make it good, against the grain of a Faroe Islands sliding into a modern money economy. Heðin Brú roots it in the real islands: the turf-roofed village, the fjord and the whale drive on its shore, the fishing boats, and the mountain outfield of the old subsistence life.

palette.note: The cold blue-grey of the North Atlantic and the fjords, the sea the whole hard Faroese life is wrung from. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
